words.definition: able to find clever ways to solve problems
private and away from other people
an image seen in a mirror or water
to frighten someone to make them do what you want
inability to believe that something is true
something that appears real but is not
satisfaction and happiness with what you have

questions.question: How did the dog survive without a master?
options: By sleeping all day
By using his wits and searching for food scraps
By stealing from other dogs

questions.question: What made this particular discovery so special?
options: It was a small, ordinary bone
It was a large, juicy bone with tender meat - his best find in months
It was made of gold

questions.question: Why was the dog worried about staying in the market square?
options: It was too noisy
Other animals might try to steal his bone
The market was closed

questions.question: Where did the dog decide to go with his bone?
options: Back to his home
A quiet grove of trees beside a gentle stream
To another market

questions.question: What was special about the stream under the bridge?
options: It was very deep and dangerous
It was crystal clear and reflected like a perfect mirror
It was full of fish

questions.question: What did the dog think he saw in the water?
options: A fish swimming
Another dog with a bone just as large as his own
A treasure chest

questions.question: What happened to the dog's attitude when he saw the reflection?
options: He became grateful for what he had
He became greedy and wanted the second bone too
He became scared and ran away

questions.question: What did greed do to the dog's thinking?
options: It made him wiser
It clouded his judgment and made him forget his peaceful plan
It had no effect

questions.question: What happened when the dog opened his mouth to bark?
options: The other dog ran away
His own bone fell from his mouth into the stream
He got the second bone

questions.question: What did the dog realize after he lost his bone?
options: That there were many other dogs around
That there had never been another dog - only his own reflection
That he could swim down to get it

questions.question: What lesson did the dog learn from his experience?
options: That he should always bark at other dogs
That greed can cause you to lose what you already have
That reflections are dangerous

questions.question: How did the dog change after this experience?
options: He became meaner to other animals
He learned to be grateful and content with what he had
He never looked at water again

informationalTexts.title: Dolphins
informationalTexts.content:
1. b) through a blowhole on top of their heads
2. b) a pod

informationalTexts.title: Tornadoes
informationalTexts.content:
1. a) when warm moist air meets cold dry air and winds create rotation
2. b) an area in the central plains where many tornadoes occur

informationalTexts.title: Pharmacists
informationalTexts.content:
1. a) dosage, instructions, and potential interactions
2. a) health advice, vaccinations, and medication guidance

informationalTexts.title: Octopuses
informationalTexts.content:
1. a) they have no bones in their bodies
2. a) they change color and texture to match their surroundings

informationalTexts.title: Glaciers
informationalTexts.content:
1. a) snow accumulates for many years and gets compressed into ice
2. a) they carve valleys, create lakes, and move boulders

informationalTexts.title: Engineers
informationalTexts.content:
1. a) they use math, science, and creativity to solve problems
2. a) safety, cost, and environmental impact

grammarLessons.content:
**Nouns**

This lesson focuses on Nouns. As you read today's story, pay attention to examples of this concept.

**Key Points:**
• Understand the concept
• Find examples in the story
• Practice using it correctly

**Practice:**
Apply what you learned by completing the exercises.

**Remember:** Nouns is an important language skill!
grammarLessons.objective: Students will understand and apply Nouns.

languageLessons.content:
**ABC Order**

This language lesson teaches ABC Order. Look for examples in today's story.

**Key Ideas:**
• Learn the concept
• Find examples
• Practice the skill

**Practice Activities:**
Complete the exercises to master this skill.

**Remember:** Practice makes perfect!
languageLessons.objective: Students will learn about ABC Order.

languageLessons.content:
Rhyming words sound the SAME at the end. They make poems and stories fun to read aloud!

**Rhyming Words:**
• cat, hat, mat, sat (all end with -at)
• play, day, say, way (all end with -ay)
• book, look, cook, took (all end with -ook)

**From today's story:**
Listen for words that rhyme as you read!

**Practice - Find the rhyme:**
1. Which word rhymes with "dog"? (log, big, cat)
2. Which word rhymes with "jump"? (run, bump, sit)
3. Which word rhymes with "bed"? (chair, red, sleep)

**Remember:** Listen to the END of words to find rhymes!
languageLessons.objective: Students will identify and create rhyming words.
